economic_notes: Michuk Forestry Limited Partnership (99.99% owned) reported revenues of $2,121,000 and net income of $30,102 in 2023. Siska Resort Lands decommissioning/remediation asset retirement obligation recognized at $96,244. Band also holds interests in BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing Limited Partnership (0.35%) and All Nations Trust Company shares.
notes: Audit is qualified due to absence of a formal budget and lack of budget presentation in financial statements — not due to a financial concern. Consolidated entities include Siska Indian Band Operating Fund, Trust Fund, Capital Fund, Social Housing Fund, Skw'akw'iyap Economic Development Corp., and Siska Traditions Society. Michuk Forestry Limited Partnership is accounted for by modified equity method as a GBE. Prior year (2022) revenue was unusually high at $7,248,292 due to $4,778,301 in Government of Canada transfers (likely one-time COVID/settlement funding), making year-over-year comparisons misleading. Asset retirement obligation of $96,244 recognized for Siska Resort Lands decommissioning. Social housing mortgage of $342,106 (CMHC) added in 2023. No schedule of remuneration included in the extracted text. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $1,728,129 + Province of BC $546,307 + CMHC $18,015 + Government of Canada $500,000 = $2,792,451.
